summary: Dubai is a city of superlatives: the world's tallest building, largest mall, and most spectacular artificial islands. This ultramodern desert metropolis seamlessly blends Emirati heritage with futuristic ambition. Luxury shopping, desert safaris, world-class dining, and year-round sunshine attract millions of visitors annually.
neighbourhoods: Downtown Dubai (Burj Khalifa, Dubai Mall, Dubai Fountain), Dubai Marina (yacht clubs, beach, restaurants), Old Dubai/Bur Dubai (souks, heritage, creek), Jumeirah (beach hotels, Jumeirah Mosque), DIFC (financial centre, fine dining, art), Palm Jumeirah (Atlantis, ultra-luxury hotels), Deira (spice souk, gold souk, traditional market)
must_do: Burj Khalifa observation deck (book ahead, sunset slot), Dubai Desert Safari (dune bashing, camel ride, BBQ dinner), Dubai Creek Abra boat ride + Gold and Spice Souks, Dubai Frame, Palm Jumeirah monorail, JBR beach, Museum of the Future, helicopter tour for palm island views
transport_tips: Dubai Metro (Red + Green lines) for major attractions. Nol card for metro, buses, trams. Uber and Careem widely used. RTA Taxis official and metered. Mall of the Emirates has metro stop. Airport: Dubai Metro Red Line directly from T1/T3 (~$4 AUD). Driving: right side, excellent roads.
safety_notes: Very safe city with zero tolerance for crime. Strict laws: no public intoxication, modest dress in traditional areas, no PDA. Ramadan: no eating/drinking in public daytime. LGBTQ+ relationships illegal. Photography of government buildings, military, and ports prohibited. Alcohol only in licensed venues.
budget_notes: Budget: $150-200 AUD/day. Mid-range: $280-400 AUD/day. Luxury: $600+ AUD/day. Mall food court $15-20 AUD. Restaurant $50-80 AUD. Fine dining $150+ AUD. Beer at a bar $20-30 AUD. Accommodation from $80 (budget hotel) to $800+ (Burj Al Arab).

sample_day_blocks.activity: Dubai Creek Abra + Gold Souk + Spice Souk + Old Dubai
Dubai Mall + Dubai Aquarium + Burj Khalifa base viewing
Burj Khalifa At The Top sunset slot + Dubai Fountain show
